title: Effect of Bougie Use on First-Attempt Success in Tracheal Intubations: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.

items.ai_reason: Identified as systematic review/meta-analysis in header and title area.
Background and Importance sections describe rationale in context of existing knowledge.
Explicit objective stated in 'Goals of This Investigation'.
Eligibility criteria section specifies inclusion/exclusion and subgroups.
Lists databases with date last searched (June 15, 2023).
Appendix E1 provides full search strategies.
Two independent reviewers screened titles/abstracts and full text with consensus.
One extractor with second reviewer cross-check; standardized form used.
Primary and secondary outcomes defined.
Lists variables extracted (setting, RSI, age, sex, etc.).
RoB 2 and modified Newcastle-Ottawa described; one assessor cross-checked.
Risk ratios with 95% CI specified.
Eligibility for syntheses described, including subgroups.
No description of handling missing summary statistics or data conversions.
Forest plots and tables described.
Random-effects DerSimonian-Laird, I2, Review Manager 5.4.1 reported.
Subgroup analyses described.
Sensitivity analyses excluding outlier (BOUGIE) described.
Funnel plot used to assess publication bias.
GRADE approach described.
Numbers reported with flow diagram (Figure 1).
Excluded studies after full-text review not individually cited with reasons.
Table 1 presents each included study characteristics.
Risk of bias presented in Appendix E3 and summarized in text.
Forest plots show study-level data with effect estimates and CIs.
Characteristics and risk of bias of contributing studies summarized.
Pooled RRs, CIs, and I2 reported.
Subgroup results presented in Table 2.
Sensitivity analysis excluding BOUGIE trial reported.
Funnel plot asymmetry assessment reported in Appendix E4.
GRADE certainty ratings reported (low, moderate).
Discussion compares findings with prior meta-analyses.
Limitations of evidence (RoB, heterogeneity) discussed.
Review limitations discussed (inability to do subgroup by experience, etc.).
Implications for practice and future research discussed.
PROSPERO CRD42023403212 registered.
Protocol available on PROSPERO; mentioned as registered there.
Post hoc difficult-airway subgroup analysis explicitly noted as amendment.
States study received no funding.
Authors report no conflicts of interest.
Data sharing statement indicates data, protocol, code available on request.
Title states 'Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis'
States aim to evaluate evidence on bougie use and first-attempt intubation success
Adults, RCTs/observational, manikin/cadaver excluded
Lists databases and search date through June 2023
GRADE mentioned for certainty but no risk of bias tool specified
Random-effects meta-analysis, GRADE
18 studies, 9,151 patients, 12 RCTs
Pooled RR 1.11 (95% CI 1.06-1.17) reported
Notes low certainty of evidence
Suggests bougie should be used first, not as rescue
No funding source mentioned
No registration provided

title: Analgesic efficacy of nitrous oxide in adults in the emergency department: A meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.

items.ai_reason: Title not provided in extracted text; identifies as meta-analysis in keywords but no explicit 'systematic review' label in title.
Introduction explains pain in ED, opioid concerns, and gap with no prior meta-analysis on N2O in adults.
Explicit objectives stated: efficacy, adverse effects, satisfaction and procedure duration.

Six databases listed with search date August 10, 2021, plus reference list checking.
Search terms presented in methods.
States two authors examined articles and agreed but does not specify independent screening procedure clearly.
Two independent authors extracted data using Excel.
Primary outcome (pain score VAS/NRS, pain relief) and secondary outcomes defined.
Lists extracted variables: author, year, sample size, age, diagnosis, N2O/O2 ratio, control method.
Cochrane risk of bias tool used by two independent authors.
OR for dichotomous, WMD for continuous, with 95% CI.
Studies grouped by comparison (placebo vs other analgesic methods).
VAS to NRS conversion, 0-100 to 0-10 scale, last time point selection described.
Forest plots and tables used.
Fixed-effect model, random-effects if I2>50%, RevMan 5.3, I2 for heterogeneity.
